LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF EMA BELL'S UNOPPOSED MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT AND CONSENT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND A SEPERATE PROPOSED CONSENT JUDGMENT. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/RBU)

AFFIDAVIT RE COMPLIANCE WITH TIT. 11 CAL. CODE REGS 3000 ET SEQ. (TRANSACTION ID # 75000976) FILED BY PLAINTIFF BELL, EMA

REMARK: UPON REVIEWING THE RECORD, THE COURT NOTES A CLERICAL ERROR. THE CLERK'S OFFICE INADVERTENTLY ADVANCED THE HEARING DATE ON PLAINTIFF EMA BELL'S MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT AND CONSENT JUDGMENT FROM DECEMBER 31, 2024, TO DECEMBER 12, 2024. THE COURT RULED UPON THE MOTION THEREOF ON DECEMBER 12, 2024. PLAINTIFF SUBMITTED A PROPOSED ORDER AND A PROPOSED CONSENT JUDGMENT ACCORDINGLY. THERE BEING NO OPPOSITION, THE RULING SHALL STAND. THE PROPOSED ORDER AND PROPOSED CONSENT JUDGMENT HAVE BEEN ROUTED FOR SIGNATURE. CLERK: V. DA FONSECA. =(302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-12-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF EMA BELL's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment.

Plaintiff's unopposed motion to approve Proposition 65 settlement and consent judgment is granted.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and a seperate proposed consent judgment.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
